user_prompt: Map the data flows for our SaaS: signup → CRM → email tool → support tool. Identify each processor.
actual_output:
# Data Flow Mapping & Processor Identification

## Data Flow Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     EU DATA SUBJECT                             │
│                  (Signs up on your SaaS)                        │
└────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                         │
                         ▼
        ┌────────────────────────────────┐
        │   YOUR SAAS PLATFORM           │
        │   (Data Controller)            │
        │                                │
        │ Collects:                      │
        │ • Email                        │
        │ • Name                         │
        │ • Password hash                │
        │ • IP address                   │
        │ • Usage events                 │
        └────────┬───────────┬───────────┘
                 │           │
        ┌────────▼──┐  ┌─────▼──────────┐
        │   CRM     │  │  EMAIL TOOL    │
        │(Processor)│  │  (Processor)   │
        │           │  │                │
        │ Stores:   │  │ Stores:        │
        │ • Email   │  │ • Email        │
        │ • Name    │  │ • Name         │
        │ • Company │  │ • Send history │
        │ • Phone   │  │ • Open/click   │
        │ • Notes   │  │   tracking     │
        └────┬──────┘  └────┬───────────┘
             │              │
             │              ▼
             │      ┌──────────────────┐
             │      │ EMAIL PROVIDER   │
             │      │ (Sub-processor)  │
             │      │                  │
             │      │ Stores:          │
             │      │ • Email content  │
             │      │ • Delivery logs  │
             └──────┼──────────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
        ┌────────────────────────────────┐
        │   SUPPORT TOOL                 │
        │   (Processor)                  │
        │                                │
        │ Stores:                        │
        │ • Email                        │
        │ • Name                         │
        │ • Support tickets              │
        │ • Chat history                 │
        │ • IP address                   │
        └────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Detailed Data Flow Analysis

### **Flow 1: Signup → Your SaaS Platform**

| Element | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Data Categories** | Email, name, password hash, IP address, signup timestamp |
| **Your Role** | **Data Controller** (you determine purposes & means) |
| **Lawful Basis** | Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) — necessary to provide service |
| **Retention** | Define: typically account lifetime + 30 days post-deletion |
| **Processor Agreements Needed** | Yes — if hosting on third-party cloud (AWS, Azure, etc.) |

**Compliance Checklist:**
- [ ] Privacy notice displayed at signup (Art. 13)
- [ ] Explicit consent for non-essential processing (e.g., marketing emails)
- [ ] Data Processing Agreement with hosting provider

---

### **Flow 2: Your SaaS → CRM (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot)**

| Element | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Data Transferred** | Email, name, company, phone, usage data, custom fields |
| **CRM Role** | **Data Processor** (Art. 28) — processes on your instructions |
| **Your Role** | **Data Controller** — you decide what data to send and why |
| **Lawful Basis** | Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) — sales/customer management |
| **Data Location** | Typically US-based (Salesforce, HubSpot) — requires adequacy decision or SCCs |
| **Retention** | CRM vendor's default (often 7 years) — should align with your policy |

**Compliance Checklist:**
- [ ] **Data Processing Agreement (DPA) signed** — mandatory under Art. 28(3)
- [ ] DPA includes: processing instructions, confidentiality, sub-processor authorization, data subject rights support
- [ ] Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) if CRM is US-based (post-Schrems II)
- [ ] Privacy notice discloses CRM as recipient (Art. 13(1)(e))

**Critical Gap:** Many SaaS teams skip DPAs with CRM vendors. This is a **high-risk violation** (potential 20M EUR fine or 4% turnover).

---

### **Flow 3: Your SaaS → Email Tool (e.g., SendGrid, Mailchimp)**

| Element | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Data Transferred** | Email, name, signup date, engagement metrics |
| **Email Tool Role** | **Data Processor** (Art. 28) |
| **Your Role** | **Data Controller** |
| **Lawful Basis** | Legitimate interest (transactional emails) + Consent (marketing emails) |
| **Data Location** | Typically US-based — requires SCCs |
| **Retention** | Email tool's default (often 1-3 years) |

**Compliance Checklist:**
- [ ] **DPA signed with email tool vendor**
- [ ] **Consent mechanism for marketing emails** (Art. 7) — opt-in, not opt-out
- [ ] SCCs in place for US-based provider
- [ ] Privacy notice discloses email tool as recipient
- [ ] Unsubscribe link on every marketing email (ePrivacy Directive)

**Sub-processor Risk:** Email tool may use third-party services (e.g., SendGrid uses AWS). Your DPA must allow sub-processor authorization.

---

### **Flow 4: Email Tool → Email Provider (e.g., AWS SES, Microsoft)**

| Element | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Data Transferred** | Email address, email content, delivery metadata |
| **Email Provider Role** | **Sub-processor** (Art. 28(2)-(4)) |
| **Your Role** | **Data Controller** (ultimately responsible) |
| **Data Location** | Typically US or EU — check vendor's data center options |
| **Retention** | Email provider's policy (often 30-90 days for logs) |

**Compliance Checklist:**
- [ ] Email tool's DPA must authorize this sub-processor
- [ ] You must have visibility into sub-processor terms (Art. 28(4))
- [ ] SCCs if sub-processor is US-based
- [ ] Email provider's privacy policy reviewed for compliance

**Note:** You are **jointly liable** with the email tool for sub-processor compliance. If the sub-processor breaches, you and the email tool are both responsible.

---

### **Flow 5: Your SaaS → Support Tool (e.g., Zendesk, Intercom)**

| Element | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Data Transferred** | Email, name, support tickets, chat history, IP address |
| **Support Tool Role** | **Data Processor** (Art. 28) |
| **Your Role** | **Data Controller** |
| **Lawful Basis** | Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) — necessary to provide support |
| **Data Location** | Typically US-based — requires SCCs |
| **Retention** | Support tool's default (often 1-7 years) — should align with your policy |

**Compliance Checklist:**
- [ ] **DPA signed with support tool vendor**
- [ ] SCCs in place for US-based provider
- [ ] Privacy notice discloses support tool as recipient
- [ ] Data retention policy documented and enforced
- [ ] Support staff trained on data subject rights (DSARs, deletions)

---

## Processor Inventory & DPA Status

| Processor | Role | Location | DPA Status | SCCs Needed? | Priority |
|-----------|------|----------|-----------|-------------|----------|
| **CRM (e.g., Salesforce)** | Processor | US | ❌ MISSING | ✅ Yes | **CRITICAL** |
judge_reasoning: The chatbot fully complied with the user's request by providing a detailed data flow map, identifying the roles of each processor, and citing relevant GDPR articles as instructed by its system prompt.
